name: domain-objects
author: ehmpathy
description: A simple, convenient way to represent domain objects, leverage domain knowledge, and add runtime validation in your code base.

scripts.fix: npm run fix:format && npm run fix:lint
scripts.build:clean: npm run build:clean:tsc && npm run build:clean:bun
scripts.build:compile: npm run build:compile:tsc && npm run build:compile:bun --if-present
scripts.build: npm run build:clean && npm run build:compile && npm run build:complete --if-present
scripts.test:commits: LAST_TAG=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 @^ 2> /dev/null || git rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD) && npx commitlint --from $LAST_TAG --to HEAD --verbose
scripts.test:lint: npm run test:lint:biome && npm run test:lint:cycles && npm run test:lint:deps
scripts.test:acceptance:locally: set -eu && npm run build && LOCALLY=true jest -c ./jest.acceptance.config.ts --forceExit --verbose --runInBand --passWithNoTests $([ "${RESNAP:-}" = "true" ] && echo '--updateSnapshot')
scripts.test: set -eu && npm run test:commits && npm run test:types && npm run test:format && npm run test:lint && npm run test:unit && npm run test:integration && npm run test:acceptance:locally
scripts.test:acceptance: set -eu && npm run build && jest -c ./jest.acceptance.config.ts --forceExit --verbose --runInBand --passWithNoTests $([ -n "${CI:-}" ] && echo '--ci') $([ "${RESNAP:-}" = "true" ] && echo '--updateSnapshot')
scripts.prepush: npm run test && npm run build
scripts.prepare:rhachet: rhachet init --keys --hooks --roles mechanic behaver driver reviewer librarian ergonomist architect
scripts.prepare: if [ -e .git ] && [ -z $CI ]; then npm run prepare:husky && npm run prepare:rhachet; fi
